content: I'm feeling really uncertain about my future as a startup founder. I've been working on my AI tools company for 3 years, and we're still in the pre-seed stage.
It's understandable to feel uncertain, especially in the early stages of a startup. What are your biggest concerns right now? Is it funding, product development, or something else?

I think it's just the lack of progress. We've been trying to get traction for a while now, and it's just not happening. I've been putting in 12-hour days, 6 days a week, and I'm starting to feel burnt out.

It sounds like you're feeling frustrated and exhausted. Have you considered taking a step back and re-evaluating your strategy? Sometimes, taking a break can help you come back to your work with fresh eyes.

I've thought about it, but I'm not sure if it's the right time. We've got a few big meetings coming up, including one with a potential investor on February 22nd. I don't want to give up now, especially since I've invested so much of my own money into the company.

It's great that you're committed to your vision. However, it's also important to prioritize your well-being. Have you talked to your team about how you're feeling, or is it just you who's feeling this way?

I've talked to my co-founder, Alex, and he's feeling the same way. We're both worried about the future of the company, and we're not sure what to do. We've been trying to balance our work with taking care of my aging parents, who live with me in Lisbon.

It's wonderful that you're supporting your parents. That can be a big responsibility, especially on top of running a startup. Have you considered seeking out mentors or advisors who can offer guidance and support?

We've thought about it, but we're not sure where to start. We've been focused on building our product and getting it to market, and we haven't had time to think about anything else. I've been trying to relax by doing some origami and woodworking in my free time.
It's great that you're finding ways to unwind. Origami and woodworking can be very therapeutic. What do you think is the most important thing for you to achieve in the next 3 months?

I think it's just to get our product launched and start generating some revenue. We've been in development for so long, and it's time to start seeing some returns. We're planning to launch on May 1st, and we're hoping to get some traction by the end of the summer.

That's a great goal to focus on. Remember to take care of yourself and your team in the process, and don't be afraid to ask for help when you need it. What's your plan for after you launch your product?

We're hoping to start expanding our team and building out our sales and marketing efforts. We've got a lot of plans, but we need to get our product out the door first. We're also thinking about moving to a bigger office space in Lisbon, maybe in the Chiado neighborhood.
That sounds like a solid plan. It's great that you're thinking ahead and preparing for growth. What do you think sets your product apart from others in the market, and how will you differentiate it?

I think it's our focus on AI and machine learning. We're using some cutting-edge technologies to build our product, and we're confident that it will be a game-changer. We're also planning to attend some conferences, including the Web Summit in November, to showcase our product and get some feedback from potential customers.
That's excellent. It's clear that you're passionate about your work and committed to your vision. I think you have a bright future ahead of you, and I'm happy to help in any way I can.
